name: Garlic (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw garlic clove, Allium sativum)

components.name: fructan (inulin-type fructooligosaccharides)
components.source: Judprasong et al. 2011 J Food Comp Anal (fructan survey); Baumgartner et al. 2000 J Agric Food Chem doi:10.1021/jf970433u
components.evidence: Garlic is one of the highest-fructan foods; ~17 g/100g fresh (studies range 7.5-17.4). Mix of FOS + fructopolysaccharides (DP up to ~38). THE dominant fermentable carbon source in garlic — dwarfs free sugars.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: IISTE FSQM free-sugar analysis of Allium; Aprifel total sugars 1.2 g
components.evidence: Main free monosaccharide; ~1-1.5 g/100g. Also the monomer released on fructan hydrolysis.

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Second free sugar; midpoint ~1.5 g/100g.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Garlic is notably low in / near-undetectable for free glucose (unlike most vegetables). Trace value used; also released on fructan/sucrose hydrolysis.

components.name: total free sugars
components.evidence: USDA total sugars = 1.00 g/100g (free sugars only; fructan reported separately and is far larger).

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Organic-acid survey of Allium (fresh garlic 600-1200 mg/100g); Springer 2025 black-garlic review doi:10.1007/s44345-025-00036-6
components.evidence: Dominant organic acid in fresh garlic; midpoint ~1.0 g/100g. Commonly OMITTED from FooDB profile.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Second organic acid; midpoint ~0.3 g/100g. Missing from current FooDB profile.

components.name: pyruvic acid
components.evidence: Enzymatically released together with allicin when alliinase acts on alliin upon crushing; intact clove lower, crushed clove much higher. Value = mid-estimate; note strong processing dependence.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: Minor organic acid.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Reported present in fresh garlic (also formic, lactic, succinic) but not reliably quantified; included with null amount.

components.name: alliin (S-allyl-L-cysteine sulfoxide)
components.source: Lawson & Hunsaker; LPI Oregon State garlic review; Ichikawa 2006 (6-14 mg/g fresh = 600-1400 mg/100g)
components.evidence: Defining garlic metabolite and a major non-protein sulfur+nitrogen pool; substrate cleaved by alliinase to allicin + pyruvate + ammonia on crushing. Midpoint ~1 g/100g.

components.name: gamma-glutamyl-S-allyl-L-cysteine
components.source: LPI Oregon State garlic review; Ichikawa 2006 (2-6 mg/g fresh = 200-600 mg/100g)
components.evidence: Principal storage form / biosynthetic precursor of alliin; another significant N/S reserve in the intact clove.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Garlic is comparatively protein-rich for a vegetable; amino-acid breakdown below. Free arginine is uniquely abundant among Allium free amino acids.

components.name: L-arginine
components.evidence: Highest-abundance amino acid in garlic; large free-arginine pool documented (stable on aging).

components.name: L-glutamate (glutamic acid)
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid by total mass.

components.name: phosphate (phosphorus)
components.evidence: Reported as elemental P; primary P source for growth.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Garlic accumulates selenium relatively well.

components.name: molybdenum
components.evidence: Genuine trace micronutrient; retained with null amount.

components.name: chromium
components.evidence: Genuine trace micronutrient; retained with null amount.

components.name: cobalt
components.evidence: Trace micronutrient (Co2+); retained with null amount.

components.name: iodine
components.evidence: Trace; retained with null amount.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Counter-ion; retained.

components.name: sulfate
components.evidence: Inorganic sulfur pool (organosulfur alliin/gamma-glutamyl peptides are the larger S reserve).

components.name: vitamin B6 (pyridoxine)
components.evidence: Garlic is unusually rich in B6.

components.name: vitamin B9 (folate)
components.evidence: Low in garlic.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Trace; vitamin A activity essentially nil.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Very low-fat matrix.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Principal fatty acid of the small garlic lipid fraction; amount not separately quantified here (total fat 0.5 g).

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Minor fatty acid.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Minor fatty acid.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Note: USDA fiber excludes fructan, which is tallied separately above as the main fermentable polysaccharide.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Genuine Allium osmolyte; retained with null amount.

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: non-nutritive toxic trace element; not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Genistein
remove.reason: soy isoflavone; not a garlic constituent (FooDB predicted/misassigned) - implausible in Allium matrix

remove.name: Isoflavones
remove.reason: soy/legume class; not characteristic of garlic (FooDB predicted)

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial-fermentation food preservative (Streptomyces), not a native garlic metabolite

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin typical of citrus/Apiaceae; implausible/predicted for garlic

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: non-natural diastereomer artifact; redundant with L-isoleucine already listed

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: trace element, not an established growth nutrient for the target microbes; FooDB elemental scan noise

missing_added: fructan (inulin-type FOS) — the dominant fermentable carbon source, previously absent
citric acid, malic acid, pyruvic acid, oxalic acid, fumaric acid (organic acids — all previously missing)
sucrose, D-fructose, D-glucose with individual amounts (only sucrose name was present, unquantified)
alliin and gamma-glutamyl-S-allyl-L-cysteine (defining organosulfur N/S pools)
quantitative amino-acid profile, full mineral panel, and vitamin panel from USDA
keep_uncovered: quercetin (garlic flavonol, genuine but low; no distinct BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169230 (Garlic, raw) — full macro/mineral/vitamin/amino-acid panel
Baumgartner et al. 2000 J Agric Food Chem doi:10.1021/jf970433u (garlic fructan/FOS molecular size)
Judprasong et al. 2011 J Food Comp Anal (fructan content of foods)
IISTE Food Science & Quality Management — free glucose/fructose/sucrose/total fructan in Allium
Springer 2025 doi:10.1007/s44345-025-00036-6 (fresh-to-black garlic physicochemical review; organic acids)
Allium organic-acid survey (citric/malic/pyruvic/oxalic in fresh garlic)
Linus Pauling Institute, Oregon State University — garlic organosulfur (alliin, gamma-glutamylcysteines)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is FRUCTAN (inulin-type FOS, ~17 g/100g) — an order of magnitude larger than the free sugars (total ~1 g/100g, fructose > sucrose >> glucose); a fructan-capable microbe grows on garlic chiefly via fructan/fructose. Organic acids (citric ~1 g, malic ~0.3 g, plus pyruvate) were entirely absent from the FooDB profile and are added here. Nitrogen/sulfur is unusual: beyond 6.4 g protein (arginine- and glutamate-rich), garlic holds large non-protein organosulfur pools (alliin ~1 g, gamma-glutamyl-S-allyl-cysteine ~0.4 g) that are its signature metabolites. Pyruvate and allicin values are strongly processing-dependent (alliinase acts on crushing). Removed 34 items: toxic/non-nutritive trace elements, a preservative (epsilon-polylysine), and implausible/predicted phytochemicals (soy isoflavones, citrus furanocoumarin).
